target.name: Mammoth Biosciences
target.founder: Jennifer Doudna, Trevor Martin, Janice Chen, Lucas Harrington

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $600M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $5.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: HistoSonics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.25B

private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series D), post-money $1.7B+

data.narrative:
Mammoth Biosciences, a Series D biotech company founded by Jennifer Doudna and colleagues, has raised $600M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private and 3 public companies in Biotech & Health, with adjacent-stage peers including HistoSonics ($2.2B), Formation Bio ($1.7B), Abridge ($5.3B), Ultima Genomics ($4.0B), and Insitro ($2.4B).

Two triangulation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $600M raised proxy and applying a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D-to-next-stage progression, yields $900M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces $2.3B (range: $1.5B–$5.3B), with HistoSonics and Formation Bio—both Series D—anchoring the lower end of the peer distribution. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 5.8x across RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) was not applied due to lack of target revenue disclosure, typical for private companies.

The triangulated range is $765M to $2.7B, with a median of $1.6B derived from equal weighting of the two applicable methods. The peer-median approach carries greater weight given the direct stage and sector alignment; stage-progression relying on a capital-raised proxy introduces downside bias. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, clinical pipeline progress, and whether Series D funding velocity warrants premium to median peer valuation.
